question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cover 2/3 of conductive side of FTO coated glass slide with semi-transparent acrylic adhesive tape
2. Coat uncovered areas of slide with zinc powder
3. Pour 2M hydrochloric acid solution in distilled water over slide to etch substrate
4. Wipe residue from exposed slide sections using cotton swab
5. Rinse etched FTO substrate with distilled water
6. Remove tape from substrate
7. Wash etched surface in 2% w/v alkaline liquid detergent concentrate solution
8. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in acetone bath
9. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in isopropanol bath
10. Treat FTO substrate in oxygen plasma cleaner for 15 minutes
11. Place etched and cleaned FTO substrate on hot plate at 450°C
12. Cover contact area with precut glass slide
13. Allow substrate to heat to 450°C
14. Mix 0.6 mL TAA with 7 mL isopropanol
15. Apply TAA solution to substrate via spray pyrolysis using air carrier gas at 450°C
16. Maintain sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
17. Cool sample to room temperature
18. Remove glass cover after substrate cooling
19. Dilute 30 nanomolar titanium oxide paste with ethanol to 2:7 weight ratio
20. Sonicate suspension for 30 minutes
21. Spin coat sample with suspension for 30 seconds at 5,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
22. Anneal titanium film at 500°C for 30 minutes to form mesoporous titanium oxide film
23. Immerse sample in 40 millimolar titanium chloride solution in distilled water
24. Treat sample at 70°C for 20 minutes
25. Anneal titanium chloride-treated sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
26. Transfer sample to nitrogen-filled glove box with <1% humidity
27. Add 1 milliliter of DMF to 553 milligrams of lead iodide
28. Heat mixture to 80 degrees Celsius while stirring continuously
29. Continue heating until lead iodide dissolves
30. Prepare 0.02 molar solution of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution
31. Apply 80 microliters of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution to substrate
32. Spin coat substrate for 30 seconds at 6,500 RPM with 4,000 RPM/s ramp rate
33. Bake thin film at 80 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
34. Dissolve 40 milligrams of methylammonium iodide in 5 milliliters of isopropanol
35. Place lead iodide coated substrate in spin coater
36. Apply 120 microliters of MAI solution to substrate
37. Allow MAI solution to sit on substrate for 45 seconds
38. Spin coat substrate with MAI for 20 seconds at 4,000 RPM
39. Anneal perovskite film at 100 degrees Celsius for 45 minutes
40. Add 1 milliliter of chlorobenzene to 72.3 milligrams of spiro-methoxyTAD
41. Shake mixture until solution appears transparent
42. Prepare stock solution of 520 milligrams of lithium TFSI in 1 milliliter of acetonitrile
43. Mix 17.5 microliters of lithium TFSI solution with 28.8 microliters of 4-tert-butylpyridine
44. Combine mixture with spiro-methoxyTAD solution
45. Spin coat sample with mixture for 30 seconds at 4,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
46. Cover sample with mask pattern for solar cell top contacts
47. Deposit 80 nanometer gold layer onto sample at 0.01 nm/s rate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve 2.6 grams of P nipam in 2 milliliters of 60% toluene and 40% hexane solution
2. Heat mixture to 60 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
3. Stir mixture until P nipam is dissolved
4. Cut filter paper into 60 millimeter diameter circle
5. Place filter paper in Buchner funnel
6. Filter solution through Buchner funnel into pre-weighed glass beaker
7. Keep beaker and contents in bell vacuum overnight
8. Weigh beaker with P nipam
9. Add isopropyl alcohol to P nipam
10. Create 50:50 weight/weight solution
11. Place two milliliters of solution on tissue culture plate surface
12. Coat plate for five minutes under UV light
13. Wash cells with two milliliters of warm PBS
14. Add three milliliters of trypsin or cleaving dissociating solution to cells
15. Incubate for five minutes
16. Add 3 mL of culture media or PBS containing 10% FBS to cells
17. Collect cells in conical tube
18. Count cells
19. Centrifuge cells at 1000 RPM for 5 minutes
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend cells in growth media
22. Plate cell suspension on 35mm thermosensitive plate at concentration for 100% confluence
23. Incubate plate at 37°C overnight
24. Collect endothelial cells
25. Disperse cells into single-cell solution
26. Deactivate trypsin enzyme with equal amount of soybean trypsin inhibitor or 10% FBS in PBS
27. Collect cells in 15 milliliter conical tube
28. Count cells with hemocytometer and calculate volume needed for patch dimensions
29. Extract 2 million endothelial cells
30. Place extracted cells in new 15 milliliter conical tube
31. Centrifuge cells for five minutes
32. Aspirate supernatant, leaving cell pellet in conical tube
33. Mix high aurin and liquid gelatin in 1:1 ratio
34. Transfer 80% of final hydrogel-gelatin volume to conical tube containing endothelial cell pellet and resuspend cells
35. Transfer suspended cells into fibrous matrix
36. Add 20% of total final volume of crosslinker
37. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour
38. Remove 35 millimeter P nypa treated plate containing cells from incubator
39. Place plate in cell culture hood
40. Aspirate media from cells
41. Add two milliliters of 37°C 6% normal gelatin to plate
42. Place metal lattice into gelatin
43. Submerge metal lattice below surface of normal gelatin
44. Place entire plate on ice for 5-7 minutes to harden gelatin
45. Use spatula to carefully separate gelatin edges from plate side
46. Lift metal lattice from plate with forceps
47. Transfer cell sheet to dish and place on fibrous matrix hydrogel combination
48. Add 2 milliliters of 37°C media to tissue culture plate
49. Incubate overnight to allow cell sheet adherence to hydrogel surface
